visualGoal: Make a direct-drive buyer see the mounting ladder as part of the base purchase, not an afterthought. Real mount/cockpit product proof at every rung; no procedural art. Every embedded image is a verified mount or cockpit so the reader literally sees rigidity climb from clamp to 8020.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The article's key folding-stand example (Rung 2). Currently text-only because no verified image exists. Needs a real photo before it can become a gearpick/shotgrid tile. ~$279, folds away, entry-DD (~5Nm) ceiling.
Referenced only as a premium 8020 example from the rig guides; low priority, text/context only.
File fanatec-csl-dd.jpg exists on disk but is NOT in verified-product-images.json, so it is treated as unverified and used text-only as a desk-candidate example.

heroImage.notes: Existing hero asset already on disk (public/images/racing/). Full 8020 rig silhouette against monitor glow — matches the mounting-ladder-climax framing.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic sim-racing garage at cool blue monitor glow showing four mounting rungs left-to-right — a desk clamp on a wooden desk, a folding wheel stand, a steel-tube cockpit, and a rigid 8020 aluminum-profile rig — each with an empty 4-bolt plate left open for a real wheelbase overlay, curator Duke Alvarez gesturing along the ladder, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, warped extrusion, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: What torque forces which mount
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center ladder chart, large readable text, racing colors, real mount photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: How flex smears force feedback
content: Rigidity = you receive what you paid for
infographicPrompts.style: Signal-before/after waveform infographic, racing palette, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez kneeling beside an 8020 aluminum-profile rig, one hand testing the wheel deck for flex, confident mechanic energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Source a real NLR Wheel Stand 2.0 photo so the article's key folding-stand rung has a body visual instead of a text-only mention?
Confirm Trak Racer TR80 US street price ($499 vs $699) and lock a single figure?
Do we want a verified base shotgrid (moza-r5/r12/simucube-2-sport/moza-r21) somewhere, or keep every embedded image a mount to preserve the ladder metaphor?
